channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Fabless chip startup backed by multi-billion Indian company wants to build a $10bn fab in India before 2027
item.description: Fabless chip startup LTSCT, backed by multi-billion Indian company, wants to build a $10bn fab facility in India.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: My dog recognizes the sounds a Waymo car makes
item.description: Most of us know the general (albeit simplified) story: Russian physiologist Ivan Pavlov used a stimulus—like a metronome—around the dogs he was studying, and soon, the hounds would start to salivate. They had learned that the sound meant food was coming.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ivanti warns customers of new critical flaw exploited in the wild
item.description: Ivanti is warning customers that a critical vulnerability that impacts its VPN appliances and other products has already been exploited in the wild by a Chinese APT group.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Many CIOs operate within a culture of fear
item.description: Many IT leaders say they work in organizations that operate with a culture of blame, making it difficult to admit mistakes and then learn from errors.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Explorando la confluencia de la tecnología y la sostenibilidad para un futuro mejor
item.description: Los esfuerzos de sostenibilidad están cobrando fuerza a escala mundial, y los gobiernos y las organizaciones están poniendo todo su empeño en la formulación y aplicación de políticas que puedan detener o incluso revertir el daño medioambiental global y crear un impacto positivo para las comunidades
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Brown University to see half a billion in federal funding halted by Trump administration
item.description: A White House official says the Trump administration is planning to halt more than half a billion dollars in contracts and grants awarded to Brown University.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne Mumphrey And Seung Min Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rust language adds trait upcasting
item.description: The Rust team has announced Rust 1.86, an update of the language that now features trait upcasting, or the ability to upcast trait objects. Rust 1.86 was announced April 3.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: GitHub upgrades tooling to help developers stop leaking secrets
item.description: Over 39 million API keys, credentials, and other secrets leaked onto GitHub’s platform last year, but an update to its scanning tool could help stop that.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 key features in Kong AI Gateway 3.10
item.description: Kong AI Gateway 3.10 introduces new capabilities designed to help organizations govern their generative AI usage more effectively, reduce hallucinations from large language models (LLMs), and boost developer productivity. We achieve this while keeping sensitive data protected and safe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to hold a meeting on possible investors to buy TikTok with possible ban at stake
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump will hold a Wednesday meeting with aides about possible investors who could buy a stake in TikTok, a deal that could potentially stop the social media site from being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: At the VA, modernizing healthcare delivery rests in patient’s control of their data
item.description: Neil Evans, acting program executive director for electronic health record modernization at VA said the VA has always had some form of electronic health record. The post At the VA, modernizing healthcare delivery rests in patient’s control of their data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Dow wants to power its Texas manufacturing complex with new nuclear reactors instead of natural gas
item.description: Dow, a major producer of chemicals and plastics, wants to use next-generation nuclear reactors for clean power and steam at a Texas manufacturing complex instead of natural gas. Dow's subsidiary, Long Mott Energy, applied Monday to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission for a construction permit.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What one giant industry likes and dislikes about DOGE
item.description: The Consumer Technology Association is a big industry that has mixed feelings about the Trump-Musk-DOGE approach to the government. The post What one giant industry likes and dislikes about DOGE first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
